clinical_history: 78 year old male. All of the following are present EXCEPT?
diagnosis: This ECG shows intrinsic sinus P waves at a rate of 65 bpm.  The patient has a dual chamber pacemaker with atrial sensing and ventricular pseudo- fusion beats--all the beats except the 3rd (last) beat in the V4-V6 display are pseudo-fusion--note that the morphology of this beat is slightly different and wider.  Q waves in V1-V3 are most likely due to underlying anterior myocardial infarction. The patient had a history of sick sinus syndrome and coronary artery disease with prior angioplasty/stenting of the left anterior descending coronary.  AV sequential pacing is ruled out as there is a clear sinus P wave and no evidence of an atrial spike preceding this.
